summary: Parallel review: FAIL (4 critical, 9 warning, 13 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: рџџЎ `bin/lib/cron.mjs:20` вЂ” `readProjectNumber` duplicates PROJECT.md I/O owned by `github.mjs:readTrackingConfig`; add `projectNumber` as a field in the `readTrackingConfig` return value to consolidate all PROJECT.md parsing in one module

рџџЎ `bin/lib/cron.mjs:178` вЂ” no warning emitted when `process.env.PATH` is empty or falsy before embedding in crontab line; silent `PATH=''` produces a broken cron job with no user-visible feedback
Both рџџЎ findings go to the backlog. Neither blocks merge.

рџџЎ `bin/lib/cron.mjs:172` вЂ” No upper bound on `--interval`; values > 59 produce `*/n` cron expressions that only match minute 0 each hour, not every N minutes as the user intends. Add validation: clamp to 59 or exit 1.
**Verdict: PASS** (one рџџЎ backlog item, no рџ”ґ criticals)

The main correctness focus of run_3 вЂ” eliminating the `staleItem.status = "ready"` in-place mutation in favor of an explicit `recoveredItems` array вЂ” is correctly implemented. The prior рџџЎ findings (mutation coupling, missing advisory note, missing test artifact) are all closed. All seven acceptance criteria hold. The `--interval` upper bound is the only new backlog item.

All seven SPEC acceptance criteria (AC1вЂ“AC7) are met with direct code-to-test mappings. The three prior рџџЎ carry-forward findings from run_2 are all closed in run_3 (test artifact stored, in-place mutation replaced with explicit `recoveredItems` array, PATH re-run note added). No рџ”ґ or рџџЎ findings. Two рџ”µ suggestions filed to backlog.

**No critical (рџ”ґ) or warning (рџџЎ) findings.** All six principal threat surfaces вЂ” crontab shell injection, prompt injection via issue title (including Unicode line separators), shell injection via `commentIssue`, concurrent-run lock, `--dry-run` flag isolation, and stale recovery вЂ” are correctly mitigated. The in-place mutation coupling flagged in prior architect reviews was fixed in task-5. Safe to merge.

рџџЎ `bin/lib/cron.mjs:20` вЂ” `readProjectNumber` is body-for-body identical to `outer-loop.mjs:117`; extract to a shared helper in `util.mjs` to avoid divergence if the project-URL regex changes (carry-forward, unresolved across all prior runs)
No рџ”ґ critical findings. All four veto categories clear:
Two prior рџџЎ carry-forwards from the architect/engineer are **closed** by run_3:
One рџџЎ remains: `readProjectNumber` duplication between `cron.mjs:20` and `outer-loop.mjs:117`. Body-for-body identical, not introduced by this feature.
